seoTitle: How to Export QuickBooks Online Chart of Accounts
metaDescription: Export your Chart of Accounts from QuickBooks Online for sharing with your accounting team or keeping compliance records.
intro: Your Chart of Accounts is the backbone of your financial reporting, and exporting it is a common need when sharing data with an accounting team or maintaining compliance records. This guide covers accessing and exporting it step by step in QuickBooks Online. It's aimed at business owners and accountants who need this data outside the platform.

steps.heading: Access the Chart of Accounts
steps.body: Navigate to the Chart of Accounts section within QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Export your accounts
steps.body: Select the export option to download the account list in a spreadsheet-friendly format.

steps.heading: Format and organize your data
steps.body: Clean up the exported file so it's easy to read and share with your accounting team.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot common issues
steps.body: If the export is incomplete or misformatted, check your filters and try exporting again.

faq.question: What format does the Chart of Accounts export as?
faq.answer: It typically exports as a spreadsheet file, such as Excel or CSV.

faq.question: Can I export just a portion of the Chart of Accounts?
faq.answer: Filtering options may let you narrow down which accounts to include before exporting.

faq.question: Why would I need to export the Chart of Accounts regularly?
faq.answer: It's often needed for compliance records, sharing with external accountants, or auditing purposes.
